rubric.factual_accuracy: 1=wrong facts, 3=mostly correct, 5=all facts match tracking data exactly
rubric.completeness: 1=missing key fields, 3=core info present, 5=all required fields + policy reference
rubric.actionability: 1=no clear ask, 3=vague resolution request, 5=specific refund/reimbursement amount stated

generated_draft.subject: Shipping Guarantee Claim — 1Z999AA10123456700 — UPS Ground 2-Day Delay
generated_draft.body:
Dear Shippo Claims Team,

We are writing regarding tracking number 1Z999AA10123456700 shipped on March 15, 2026 via UPS Ground. Per UPS Ground SLA, delivery was guaranteed by March 19, 2026. The package was delivered on March 21, 2026 — 2 business days beyond the guaranteed date.

Per UPS Service Guarantee policy, we respectfully request reimbursement of the shipping fee ($12.50) for this delayed shipment.

Tracking Summary:
- March 15: Package picked up
- March 19: Delivery attempted — no access (guaranteed delivery date)
- March 21: Delivered

Please process this claim at your earliest convenience. We are happy to provide any additional documentation.

Best regards,
REBLOOM Logistics

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Professional tone appropriate for first attempt. All dates and amounts accurate. Specific dollar amount stated. Minor: could reference UPS Ground guarantee policy number for stronger claim.

generated_draft.subject: Shipping Guarantee Claim — 1Z999AA10123456701 — UPS 2-Day 3-Day Delay
generated_draft.body:

While we note a weather delay was recorded at Louisville hub, the UPS Service Guarantee applies unless weather events meet specific exemption criteria. We request review of this shipment for guarantee refund of $24.99.

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Good handling of the weather caveat — acknowledges it without conceding. Slightly generic in structure. Dollar amount clearly stated.

generated_draft.subject: Damage Claim — 774899172100 — FedEx Ground
generated_draft.body:
Dear FedEx Claims Team,

We are filing a damage claim for tracking number 774899172100, shipped March 18, 2026 via FedEx Ground. Per your tracking records, damage was recorded at the Memphis facility on March 20, 2026.

The shipment contained perishable floral arrangements with a declared value of $89.99. Due to the in-transit damage, the contents were non-deliverable.

We respectfully request reimbursement of $89.99 per FedEx's declared value coverage policy. Documentation available upon request.

Best regards,
REBLOOM Logistics

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Good damage claim structure. Correctly mentions declared value. Could be stronger on specific reimbursement path (portal vs email).

generated_draft.subject: Damage Claim — 774899172102 — Customer Refused Damaged Package
generated_draft.body:
Dear FedEx Claims Team,

We are submitting a damage claim for tracking 774899172102, shipped April 1, 2026. The customer refused delivery upon inspection of a visibly damaged package — a reasonable action given the condition of the shipment upon arrival.

Shipment value: $199.99. We request full reimbursement per FedEx damage coverage policy. The customer's refusal does not constitute a return request but rather confirmation of carrier-caused damage.

Available documentation: delivery exception record, customer communication, order details.

Best regards,
REBLOOM Logistics

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Excellent. Proactively addresses the 'refused delivery' nuance (critical for this claim type). Documentation list included. Clear ask. Best in category.

generated_draft.subject: RESUBMISSION: Shipping Guarantee Claim — 1Z999AA10123456720
generated_draft.body:
Dear Shippo Claims Team,

We are resubmitting claim for tracking 1Z999AA10123456720. Our records show guaranteed delivery of March 7, 2026, with actual delivery on March 9 — 2 business days late per UPS Ground SLA.

We respectfully disagree with the denial basis. Per UPS tracking data (attached), the package was scanned at destination facility on March 7 but not delivered until March 9 — confirming the delay.

This is a legitimate service guarantee claim and we request this be reviewed by a senior claims specialist. Reimbursement requested: $24.99.

REBLOOM Logistics

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Good firm tone — assertive without being hostile. Correctly escalates to 'senior claims specialist'. Evidence-based pushback. Clear ask maintained.

generated_draft.body:

REBLOOM Logistics

scenarios.judge_reasoning: Appropriately firm on a disputed filing window claim. Math is correct (13 days = within window). Could be stronger with original submission confirmation number.
